response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a genuine track record in developer tooling — including hands-on IDE development at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack for this role is missing. There is no meaningful Kotlin or Spring experience, Java appears only peripherally, and most recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM ecosystem. The required deep JVM expertise and enterprise-framework background are not evidenced, making the fit weak despite strong general engineering credentials.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Demonstrated passion for language tooling (test runner, assertion library, web server in D)
Broad, long-tenured engineering career with craftsmanship focus (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure
No Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise JVM framework background
Recent work is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, far from JVM tooling
